name: Grok-3 mini
note: Cost-efficient reasoning model, ~90% cheaper than Grok 3; exposes a low/high reasoning_effort API parameter. Deprecated Feb 28, 2026 and again in the May 15, 2026 retirement wave.

results.notes: Aggregator page cites Artificial Analysis / Hugging Face as the underlying data source; reasoning-effort level not specified by the aggregator.
Humanity's Last Exam score, via Artificial Analysis per the aggregator page.
Instruction-following score, via Artificial Analysis per the aggregator page.

SimpleQA 21.7% for 'Grok 3 mini Beta', from xAI's own Feb 17, 2025 launch-blog benchmark table (same table already used as the self-reported source for the sibling Grok-3 Standard file's SimpleQA=43.6% row). Confirmed via direct fetch of medium.com/@bernardloki, which literally states 'Grok 3 mini Beta: 21.7%' for SimpleQA alongside the matching 43.6% figure for the full Grok 3 Beta. Not previously tracked for this model (simpleqa slug absent from grok-3-mini.json).

Official PutnamBench leaderboard entry 'Grok-3-mini': solved 0 of 672 Lean 4 problems pass@1, evaluated via FTPEvals (commit 0da3032, cost <$1; model config grok3-mini.yaml -> API model 'grok-3-mini-beta', matching this corpus's single Grok-3 mini entry, released 2025-02-17). Added in leaderboard commit 5c2d86be (2025-04-12).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real, launched same Feb 17, 2025 event as Grok 3. All 4 result rows literally confirmed on designforonline.com via direct fetch ('GPQA Diamond 79.1%', 'MATH 500 99.2%', 'AIME 2025 84.7%', 'LiveCodeBench 69.6%'). Pricing $0.30/$0.50 per 1M tokens confirmed independently. Noted that xAI's own launch-day announcement separately advertised a higher AIME-2024/cons@64 figure (95.8%) not present in this record — left as-is since only the cited row's number needed literal confirmation, which it has.

2026-07-16: Checked the 4 missing core benchmarks (mmlu, humaneval, swe-bench, mmmu) against all three source tiers. (1) Official/self-reported: the already-cited designforonline.com aggregation of xAI's Feb 17, 2025 launch table lists GPQA Diamond, MATH-500, AIME 2025, LiveCodeBench, MMLU-Pro, HLE, IFBench, SciCode, TerminalBench-Hard, tau2-Bench, and LCR for Grok-3 mini -- with no MMLU, HumanEval, SWE-bench, or MMMU row present. (2) Independent leaderboards: Vals.ai's grok-3-mini-fast-high-reasoning page shows only MMLU-Pro/GPQA Diamond/LiveCodeBench/CorpFin/TaxEval/LegalBench, no HumanEval/SWE-bench/MMMU; Epoch AI's grok-3-mini page (fetched via rendered browser, since its benchmark table is JS-populated) explicitly states "No multimodal benchmarks evaluated for this model" under the multimodal category, and its math/science and coding categories list MATH Level 5, OTIS Mock AIME, GPQA Diamond, FrontierMath, WeirdML, and Aider Polyglot -- but no MMLU, HumanEval, or SWE-bench entries anywhere; Artificial Analysis's current Intelligence Index v4.1 for grok-3-mini-reasoning has moved on to GDPval-AA, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, and AA-LCR as its component benchmarks and no longer tracks legacy MMLU/HumanEval at all, nor does it show SWE-bench or MMMU; Vals.ai's dedicated SWE-bench Verified leaderboard has no Grok-3-mini entry (only Grok 4.5 appears for xAI). (3) Sibling cross-reference: grok-3-standard.json and grok-3-think.json do carry self-reported MMMU scores sourced from xAI's own launch-blog chart, but that chart compares the Standard and Think configurations against external models (Gemini 2.0 Flash Thinking, o1) -- neither sibling's notes describe a "Grok 3 mini" bar in it, and this model's own text-only/vision:false capability flags are independently corroborated by Epoch AI's explicit "no multimodal benchmarks evaluated" statement for this exact model. Conclusion: MMLU, HumanEval, SWE-bench, and MMMU were never published for Grok-3 mini specifically -- xAI released MMLU-Pro (not plain MMLU) and LiveCodeBench (not HumanEval) as this variant's launch benchmarks, no agentic-coding/SWE-bench eval was run for it at the time, and no vision/multimodal eval exists for a text-only model. All 4 gaps resolved as omitted_no_evidence; no existing result rows were altered.

2026-07-18: Re-chased the swe-bench and mmmu gaps with a fresh research pass (following on from the 2026-07-16 pass), per instructions to search harder before concluding absence on current-gen benchmarks.

SWE-BENCH VERIFIED -- checked 5 of the 6 required methods:
(1) Official launch materials: x.ai/news/grok-3 blocked fetch this round (403) and docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-3-mini 404'd; no new official table surfaced beyond the designforonline.com aggregation of the launch table already on file, which lists no SWE-bench row.
(2) Independent trackers: fetched swebench.com's own official Verified leaderboard directly -- only "Grok 4.5 -- 86.60%" appears under xAI; no Grok-3-mini or Grok-3 entry at all. Also fetched Vals.ai's dedicated SWE-bench Verified leaderboard -- same result, only Grok 4.5 listed for xAI. Re-fetched Epoch AI's grok-3-mini model page (epoch.ai/models/grok-3-mini): its benchmark section shows only a single "Reasoning" category (Epoch Capabilities Index 141, rank 74/211 across effort settings) with no SWE-bench evaluation named anywhere.
(3) Academic: searched arXiv (site:arxiv.org and general queries) for any paper using grok-3-mini as a SWE-bench baseline -- none found; returned papers covered unrelated SWE-bench variants (SWE-MiniSandbox, SWE-Bench++, SWE-Debate, Holistic Agent Leaderboard) that do not benchmark this model.
(4) GitHub eval-harness logs: searched for grok-3-mini SWE-bench results.json / harness output -- none found.
(5) Sibling-model comparison: Artificial Analysis's grok-4-vs-grok-3-mini-reasoning comparison page lists SWE-bench-adjacent categories among ~22 tracked benchmarks but shows "No data available" for grok-3-mini-reasoning on every one. A third-party aggregator (pricepertoken.com/compare/xai-grok-3-mini-vs-xai-grok-4) cited an unlabeled "coding benchmarks" score of 25.2 for Grok-3 Mini vs. 40.5 for Grok 4, but the page could not be re-fetched to verify whether this figure is SWE-bench, a composite index, or something else (403 on retry), so it was not used as a citable SWE-bench source.
Conclusion: no credible, verifiable SWE-bench Verified score for Grok-3 mini specifically exists on any checked source as of this pass. Reconfirmed omitted_no_evidence.

MMMU -- reconfirmed architecturally inapplicable. The corpus's own capabilities.vision=false is independently corroborated again this pass by: (a) multiple web sources explicitly describing Grok-3-mini as text-only with no image/vision support, contrasted against full Grok 3/Grok 4's multimodal capability (TechTarget's Grok-3 explainer, Oracle's Grok-3-mini docs mirror, and xAI's own model-description language surfaced via search); (b) Epoch AI's explicit "no multimodal benchmarks evaluated" statement for this exact model (established 2026-07-16, still holds -- the re-fetched epoch.ai/models/grok-3-mini page this pass shows no multimodal category at all, consistent with that finding); (c) Artificial Analysis's grok-4-vs-grok-3-mini-reasoning comparison page showing no MMMU data point for grok-3-mini. One low-quality SEO aggregator, deepranking.ai/llm-models/grok-3-mini, claims an MMMU score of "69.4%" for Grok-3 Mini in the very same sentence where it says the model "lacks ... advanced multimodal fluency" -- an internally self-contradictory, uncited claim from a content-mill site with no traceable original source. It directly conflicts with every authoritative modality confirmation above and with the corpus's own confirmed vision:false flag, so it was rejected as not credible and not added. MMMU gap reconfirmed as omitted_no_evidence, reason: architecturally inapplicable (text-only model, no vision capability, independently corroborated across multiple sources including Epoch AI's explicit statement).
